_meta.full_name: Mini-Markers: A Brief Version of Goldberg's Unipolar Big-Five Markers (40 trait-adjective Big Five markers) (Korean translation)
_meta.license: Copyrighted instrument published in Saucier, G. (1994), Journal of Personality Assessment (Taylor & Francis). The author, Gerard Saucier, distributes the full instrument and scoring key free of charge on his University of Oregon faculty page for non-commercial research/educational use (the page also invites researchers to contact him for normative values). The Mini-Markers is a selected subset of Goldberg's (1992) Big-Five adjective markers, which follow the public-domain/IPIP tradition; the items themselves are single, common English trait adjectives that are not individually copyrightable. Free for non-commercial research and educational use; no commercial license is established.
_meta.reuse_notes: 한국어 번역(파생물). 비상업 1단계용. LLM 번역이며 검증된 한국어 표준검사가 아님 — 실사용 전 재검수 권장. 라이선스는 원본을 따르되 번역은 파생물이므로 CC-SA/연구용 조건이 그대로 적용됨.
_meta.attribution: Saucier, G. (1994). Mini-Markers: A brief version of Goldberg's unipolar Big-Five markers. Journal of Personality Assessment, 63(3), 506-516. Item list and scoring key (Table 3: Varimax-Rotated Factor Loadings of 40 Mini-Marker Scale Items, N = 636) retrieved verbatim from the author's University of Oregon faculty page: https://pages.uoregon.edu/gsaucier/gsau41.pdf · 한국어 번역: deepinme 번역 워크플로우(2026-07, LLM).
_meta.scoring_notes: 40 unipolar trait adjectives, 8 per Big-Five scale. Standard instruction ("How Accurately Can You Describe Yourself?") asks respondents to write a number indicating how accurately each trait describes them on a 9-point accuracy rating scale (1 = Extremely Inaccurate ... 9 = Extremely Accurate). Scoring per the author's key: negatively-loading (reverse-keyed) items are reflected (9->1, 8->2, ... 1->9) before summing; the author's preferred method reflects reverse items, sums each scale's 8 items, and divides by 8 for the scale mean. The "keyed" field gives the sign of each item's highest varimax loading on its assigned factor from Saucier's Table 3. Factor labels (Saucier): I = Extraversion, II = Agreeableness, III = Conscientiousness, IV = Emotional Stability, V = Intellect or Openness. For Factor IV (Emotional Stability), "+" keyed items (Unenvious, Relaxed) indicate emotional stability and "-" keyed items indicate neuroticism.
_meta.cautions: All 40 items and their factor/keying were extracted verbatim from Saucier's own instrument PDF (form plus Table 3), not reconstructed from memory. Notes: (1) The 9-point rating-scale anchor legend was not present as extractable text in the PDF (it appears to have been a graphic); the 1-9 accuracy format is confirmed both by the instrument's own scoring instructions, which reference reflecting values "[9-->1, 1-->9]", and by the Saucier (1994) / Goldberg marker tradition. (2) In Table 3 the item is spelled "Extroverted" while the administered self-report form prints "Extraverted"; the verbatim item as administered is "Extraverted". (3) Factor IV is labeled "Emotional Stability" by Saucier; much secondary literature labels this pole "Neuroticism" and lists Moody/Jealous/etc. as positively keyed on Neuroticism -- keying here is expressed relative to Saucier's Emotional Stability pole (sign of the published loading). (4) This is the original 1994 U.S. Mini-Markers; the "International English Mini-Markers" (Thompson, 2008) is a separate, revised 40-item instrument and is NOT reproduced here. (5) "facet" is intentionally blank: the Mini-Markers is a domain-level measure with no facet structure. | 번역본: 문항 순서·채점방향(keyed)·factor/facet은 원본과 동일. text_en에 원문 병기.

translation.method: LLM faithful translation (deepinme workflow, 2026-07) — 충실 번역이며 '쉬운말 리라이팅'은 별도 단계
translation.status: working draft — NOT a validated Korean instrument

qa.reviewer_overall: Clean, high-quality set. All 40 source items are translated one-to-one (count matches), and every item uses the correct Korean attributive/descriptive adjective form suited to self-report trait rating, with no 번역투 — naturalness is high. Fidelity is high: the measured meaning is preserved and important lexical distinctions are maintained (시기심 vs 질투심 for Envious/Jealous; the Efficient/Inefficient, Creative/Uncreative, Intellectual/Unintellectual, Envious/Unenvious, Sympathetic/Unsympathetic antonym pairs; and the Bashful/Shy/Quiet/Withdrawn cluster are all differentiated). Only minor nuance drifts remain: #40 위축된 leans toward a Neuroticism 'cowed' reading rather than social withdrawal; #34 발끈하는 shifts Touchy from oversensitivity toward anger; #14 안절부절못하는 gives restlessness where Fretful implies worry; #23 사색적인 slightly softens 'Philosophical.' None are meaning-breaking; the set is usable as-is with small optional refinements.

issues.problem: 'Withdrawn' is a low-Extraversion marker (socially withdrawn, keeps to oneself). The second gloss 위축된 connotes being intimidated/cowed/emotionally diminished, which reads as a Neuroticism trait rather than social withdrawal.
issues.suggestion: Keep 소극적인 and pair with a social-withdrawal sense: 소극적인 / 내성적인 (or 사람들과 잘 어울리지 않는); drop or de-emphasize 위축된.

issues.problem: 'Touchy' means oversensitive / easily offended (thin-skinned). 쉽게 발끈하는 emphasizes flaring up in anger, which drifts toward irritability and overlaps with Temperamental(33).
issues.suggestion: 쉽게 상처받는 / (신경이) 예민한 to capture the easily-offended sense; e.g. 쉽게 발끈하는 / 예민한.

issues.problem: 'Fretful' centers on anxious worrying/fretting (Emotional Stability marker). 안절부절못하는 conveys behavioral restlessness (can't sit still) more than internal worry.
issues.suggestion: 걱정이 많은 / 조바심 내는, optionally combined: 걱정이 많은 / 안절부절못하는.

issues.problem: Minor: 'Philosophical' (Intellect/Openness marker) is rendered as 사색적인 (contemplative/reflective), a defensible trait reading but a slight drift from the literal 철학적인.
issues.suggestion: Acceptable as-is; if closer fidelity is wanted, 사색적인 / 철학적인.

verification.summary: 번역 QA(2차 에이전트): fidelity=high, naturalness=natural, count_match=True, issues=4
